headline: Did State Street’s Rejected Independent-Chair Proposal Reframe Its Governance Trade-offs for Investors in STT?
summary: In May 2026, State Street Corporation’s shareholders voted on a proposal to amend the by-laws to require an independent board chair at the next CEO transition, but the measure was ultimately not approved despite appearing in the meeting materials as having been considered. This governance debate, alongside ongoing preferred dividend payments across multiple series, highlights how investors are weighing board independence against capital return priorities at State Street. We’ll now examine...

headline: Vanguard’s VOO Hits $1 Trillion of Assets in ETF Industry First
summary: (Bloomberg) -- A seemingly endless appetite for buying US stock dips has propelled Vanguard Group’s S&P 500-tracking ETF past $1 trillion in assets, making it the first fund of its kind to reach a milestone once thought unimaginable for the ETF industry.Most Read from BloombergTrump Begins Rebuilding His Tariff Wall, Citing Forced LaborRussia Finance Officials Tell Putin War Spending Is UnaffordableTrump to Get Audit Immunity as $1.8 Billion Fund in DoubtTikTok Billionaire Overtakes Mukesh Amban

headline: ARKX vs. XAR: Two ETFs Worthy of Consideration
summary: One fund leans into disruptive tech, while the other sticks to industry stalwarts—cost, volatility, and sector focus set them apart for investors.
